---
title: "Revolution Medicines Feasibility Report"
subtitle: "NSCLC 2L therapies in KRAS G12D+"
author: "Alex Paynter"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%d %b %Y')`"
editor_options:
  quarto:
    chunk_output_type: console
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    theme: sandstone 
execute:
  echo: false
  include: false
  warning: false
  message: false
  fig.width: 7
  fig.height: 5
---
  
```{r}
library(fs)
library(purrr)
library(here)
purrr::walk(.x = fs::dir_ls(here("R")), .f = source)
```


```{r}
theme_gtsummary_compact(font_size = 12)
theme_gtsummary_language("en", big.mark = "") 
```


## Introduction

This report addresses the feasibility of forming a cohort. The target population is non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) patients who have received second line therapy.  Additionally, they must have received anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 therapy and platinum chemotherapy before the second line.  We will leverage the existing curation in the BPC (v3.1-consortium) cohort.  Additional lung cancer patients exist in main GENIE which may be used to expand the cohort with custom curation.

## Attrition table

The following table applies inclusion criteria to GENIE participants, showing how many are eliminated at each step.  We'll show the table first, and detailed explanations follow below.  Each **numbered** step in the table is cumulative, meaning that the third row states the number who meet the conditions in the first, second and third rows, and so on.  The exception is the last step, [6], which is broken down from step [5] in three ways.

```{r}
flow_track <- readr::read_rds(
  here('data', 'flow_track.rds')
)

flow_track_ft <- flow_track %>%
  mutate(n = map_int(pt_dat, nrow)) %>%
  select(message, n) %>%
  flextable() %>%
  set_header_labels(message = "Condition", n = "n") %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  align(j = "n", align = "right") %>%
  fontsize(size = 10) %>%
  autofit() 
```

```{r}
#| include: true
flow_track_ft
```

```{r}
drug_string_lists <- readr::read_rds(here('data', 'drug_string_matches.rds'))
```


Details:

- **BPC NSCLC v3.1** - The biopharma collaborative NSCLC cancer dataset, version 3.1-consortium.
- **US sites only** - UHN (Canada) is removed from the data.
- **KRAS G12D+** - These people had the mutation of interest in at least one NGS test.
- **Metastatic** - These people were diagnosed as metastatic at some point.  Lines of therapy usually begin at metastasis, so this is effectively required unless a different meaning was intended.
- **Had 2L therapy** - These people had at least two lines on or after the metastatic diagnosis.  We eliminate exact duplicates from line consideration (e.g. Gem-Carbo followed by Gem-Carbo is not a new line).
- **2L contains chemo** - These people had at least one chemo agent in their second line, which we define as {`r paste(drug_string_lists$chemo, collapse = ', ')`}
- **2L contains anti-PD-(L)1** These people had at least one anti-PD-1 or anti-PD-L1 agent in their second line, which we define as {`r paste(drug_string_lists$anti_pd1, collapse = ', ')`}.
- **2L contains both** regimen has both and pd-(l)1 and chemo.


## Full 2L + inclusion breakdown

The following table counts the number of people who had each regimen in the table above at step [5]:

```{r}
flow_track %>% 
  filter(str_detect(message, "Had 2L therapy")) %>%
  pull(all_dat) %>%
  .[[1]] %>%
  count(regimen_drugs, sort = T) %>%
  flextable(.) %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  align(j = "n", align = "right") %>%
  fontsize(size = 10) %>%
  autofit() 
```


## Additional curation

The GENIE project contains many NSCLC cases which have not undergone deep curation, specifically there are 33,900 NSCLC patients.  3549 have already been fully, curated which leaves 30,351 cases available to curate.  A projected expectation is **an additional 26 or so cases in that group which meet all criteria above, for a total of 29**.  

Curation of this group could be fairly targeted because KRAS G12D status is known for all GENIE patients.

```{r}
lot <- readr::read_rds(here('data', 'lot.rds'))
```


## Second line therapies

A separate request was made to list the most common second line therapies in our NSCLC cohort overall.  This is, not applying criteria above.  We use the same line algorithm as above: second line means second regimen on or after metastatic diagnosis, excepting regimens that are exactly the same as the previous one.

Here are the top 20 most common second line therapies:

```{r}
#| include: true
lot %>%
  filter(line_of_therapy %in% 2) %>%
  count(regimen_drugs, sort = T) %>%
  slice(1:20) %>%
  flextable(.) %>%
  theme_booktabs() %>%
  align(j = "n", align = "right") %>%
  fontsize(size = 10) %>%
  autofit() 
```

"Investigational Drug" is a masked therapy, often part of a clinical trial.
